HSC Survey Results

Scots Bay Survey # 2 Results from 05/24/2026

Survey Details

3 commercial purse seiners conducted this fishing survey on 05/24/2026 starting at 19:30:00, which was at high tide.

3 vessels ran various transects each in the Main Survey Box, the Lady Melissa ran two lines in the Eastern Survey Box, and the Brunswick Provider ran four lines in the Northern Survey Box.

There was a plankton tow and replicate conducted and CTD cast by Lady Melissa.

No tags were applied during this survey.

Samples were obtained for target strength estimation.

Tides Tables

Tide Schedule for Margaretsville NS, Station #315m Source: www.waterlevels.gc.ca

Acoustic Equipment

Each vessel was equipped with a Simrad ES38-18/200-18C combi (2-in-1) transducer prior to the survey that contains a 38kHz split beam and 200 kHz single beam transducer. Data was logged to the computer hard-drive for post-processing.

Survey Results

Main Results

The results are subject to change as DFO will re-evaluate data editing, area estimation and apply a target strength.

Preliminary biomass prediction for Scots Bay using standardized parallel transects is 61,138mt, and with turnover applied 57,169mt.

Vessel Performance

Speed

Average vessel speed was 8.49. This is above the 8 knot requirement; vessels are urged to slow down to prevent backscatter noise from interfering with the acoustic data.

The speed for each transect are rudimentary and are calculated based on distance. If a significant amount of distance is cut, the speed will appear slower.

Distance

No significant distance was cut from the planned lines; vessels covered the planned area well.
